evals.prompt: Write a PHPUnit test for a PHP class
evals.expected_output: class OrderServiceTest extends TestCase { public function test_creates_order_with_valid_data(): void { $service = new OrderService(); $result = $service->create(['title' => 'Test']); $this->assertSame('Test', $result->title); } }

evals.prompt: Use Pest for testing a PHP application
evals.expected_output: it('creates an order', function() { $result = (new OrderService())->create(['title' => 'Test']); expect($result->title)->toBe('Test')->and($result->id)->not->toBeNull(); }); cleaner than PHPUnit

evals.prompt: Mock dependencies in PHP unit tests
Set up data providers in PHPUnit
evals.expected_output: #[DataProvider('statusProvider')] public function test_status(string $input, bool $expected): void; public static function statusProvider(): array { return [['active', true], ['inactive', false]]; }

evals.prompt: Follow TDD in a PHP project
evals.expected_output: Red: write failing test for createOrder(); Green: implement minimal OrderService::create() to pass; Refactor: extract validation logic; repeat; use assertSame (strict) over assertEquals

evals.prompt: Use assertSame vs assertEquals in PHPUnit
evals.expected_output: assertSame checks type + value (===): assertSame(1, 1, 'Must be integer'); assertEquals only checks value (==): assertEquals('1', 1) passes; prefer assertSame to avoid type coercion surprises

should_not_trigger: Jest tests for JavaScript React components
JUnit 5 tests in a Java Spring Boot app
Vitest tests for a Next.js application
